commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the embedded-run source-reply mode mismatch failure, resets command lanes with queued work after drained stale recovery, updates focused tests, and adds a changelog entry for a Telegram foreground wake-up regression.

PR surface: Source -1, Tests +41, Docs +1. Total +41 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. Source inspection shows the relevant guard and lane-release paths, and the PR body supplies equivalent-hotfix logs, but not a current-main or this-branch reproduction of the exact Telegram foreground wake-up failure.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer judgment on the delivery-mode contract plus exact Telegram branch proof; this is not a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: fix(codex): preserve telegram foreground run release
target.body:
## Summary
- continue steering active embedded runs when a later source reply delivery mode drifts to `message_tool_only` instead of hard-failing the queue message
- release command lanes with queued work after stale embedded-run recovery even when the aborted run drained successfully
- cover the Gwakga Telegram foreground wake-up regression with focused unit tests and changelog entry

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram-origin foreground embedded runs on vps7/Gwakga could complete internal work but leave the operator waiting for a wake-up because the run drifted away from the direct Telegram lane and stale recovery ended with `released=0`.
- Real environment tested: vps7/Gwakga root-user OpenClaw Gateway, Telegram channel `@jinon_gwakga_bot`, package `OpenClaw 2026.5.26`, direct operator chat `7360371189`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the equivalent local runtime guard to the installed vps7 dist files, restarted `openclaw-gateway.service`, checked `/healthz`, `/readyz`, and Telegram channel status, then ran a live delivery smoke with `openclaw agent --deliver --reply-channel telegram --reply-to 7360371189`.
- After-fix evidence: copied live output / redacted runtime log evidence from vps7:

```text
node --check /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/dispatch-BlnsVH60.js: passed
node --check /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime-DDb7eHkU.js: passed
/healthz: live
/readyz: ready=true eventLoop.degraded=false
openclaw channels status: Telegram default enabled/configured/running/connected webhook mode
openclaw agent --deliver --reply-channel telegram --reply-to 7360371189: deliveryStatus.status=sent
Gateway log: Telegram outbound message id 8615
post-patch log review: no new queued_behind*, stuck session, released=0, or final reply suppressed lines
```

- Observed result after fix: Gwakga accepted a live Telegram outbound delivery after restart and the post-patch log window did not reproduce the foreground wake-up stall or `released=0` release failure.
- What was not tested: this PR branch was not installed as an npm package on vps7; the live proof used the equivalent local dist hotfix. CI and unit/type/lint checks cover the source-form implementation proposed here.

## Tests
- `pnpm lint`
- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.test.ts`

## Context
This upstreams the durable parts of the vps7/Gwakga runtime hotfix for Telegram foreground embedded-run release stalls. The broader local fallback that automatically delivered any unmirrored `message_tool_only` final was intentionally omitted because it breaks existing private-final policy; upstream already has metadata-gated source reply delivery for actual message-tool sends.
